name: Fernando Mendoza
summary: Heisman winner who led Indiana to a national title with 41 TDs and only 6 INTs. Elite accuracy and pocket presence in a pro-style offense.
fitReason: Top QB-needy teams picking early enough to secure the consensus QB1 of the class.

name: Jeremiyah Love
summary: Consensus RB1 with sprinter speed, elite vision, and three-down ability. Rushed for 1,372 yards and 18 TDs in 2025 at Notre Dame.
fitReason: Teams with RB as a top need or who value a dynamic three-down weapon in their offensive scheme.

name: Sonny Styles
risks: Still developing as a pure linebacker
summary: Former safety turned linebacker with generational size-speed combination. 43.5-inch vertical and 4.46 speed at 244 pounds make him a scheme-versatile defensive weapon.
fitReason: Teams needing a versatile LB who can cover, blitz, and spy mobile QBs with elite athleticism.

name: David Bailey
risks: Needs to add mass for NFL edge
summary: Dominant pass rusher with 14.5 sacks and 23 TFLs in 2025. Combines power, speed, and relentless motor to terrorize offensive tackles.
fitReason: Teams with EDGE as a top draft need who want an immediate impact pass rusher.

name: Arvell Reese
summary: Versatile hybrid defender with linebacker instincts and edge-rusher closing speed. Micah Parsons comp due to his ability to play multiple positions at an elite level.
fitReason: Teams that deploy versatile defensive pieces and need a chess piece who can rush the passer and drop into coverage.

name: Carnell Tate
risks: Overshadowed by Jeremiah Smith at Ohio State
summary: Smooth route runner with great hands and body control who averaged 17.2 yards per catch at Ohio State. Produced big plays consistently despite sharing targets.
fitReason: Teams needing a polished WR1 who can win at all three levels of the field and produce immediately.

name: Rueben Bain Jr.
risks: Undersized for every-down EDGE at 250 lbs
summary: Dynamic pass rusher with an elite 23.5% pass-rush win rate. Versatile enough to rush from the edge and kick inside on passing downs.
fitReason: Teams that need an immediate pass-rush upgrade and value scheme versatility on the defensive line.

name: Caleb Downs
summary: Instinctive safety who plays faster than his timed speed thanks to elite football IQ. Freshman sensation at Alabama who transferred to Ohio State and continued dominating.
fitReason: Teams needing a safety who can be the quarterback of the secondary and impact both run and pass defense.

name: Mansoor Delane
summary: Lockdown corner with an elite 90.7 PFF coverage grade who allowed completions on just 40% of targets. Combines ideal size with shutdown ability.
fitReason: Teams with CB as a top need who want a true lockdown corner to anchor the secondary.

name: Francis Mauigoa
risks: May be limited to right tackle
summary: Massive and powerful right tackle prospect who projects as an immediate starter with All-Pro upside. Dominant in the run game with the potential to play left tackle eventually.
fitReason: Teams with OL as a top need who want a day-one starter at tackle to anchor the offensive line.

name: Makai Lemon
summary: Biletnikoff Award finalist who led the Big Ten with 79 receptions for 1,156 yards and 11 TDs. Reliable slot/outside weapon with elite route running.
fitReason: Teams needing a high-volume pass catcher who can win from the slot and outside with route craft rather than pure speed.

name: Olaivavega Ioane
strengths: Most pro-ready interior lineman in class
Wide base and powerful drive blocking
risks: Limited to guard — may not have tackle versatility
summary: The most NFL-ready interior lineman in the draft. Wide base, strong legs, and excellent drive blocking make him a plug-and-play starter at guard.
fitReason: Teams with OL as a top need who want an immediate starter at guard to solidify the interior.

name: Kenyon Sadiq
summary: Athletic freak who ran the fastest 40 by a tight end in combine history (4.39) with a 43.5-inch vertical. Limited college production but elite physical tools project massive NFL upside.
fitReason: Teams that can develop a raw but physically dominant TE into a matchup nightmare in their passing game.

name: Spencer Fano
strengths: Versatile — can play tackle or guard
risks: Short arms (32 1/8 inches) may force move inside
May not be a true left tackle
summary: Technically polished offensive lineman who ran a 4.91 40 at 311 pounds. Arm length concerns may push him inside, but his technique and athleticism make him a safe first-round pick.
fitReason: Teams needing OL help who value a technically sound, versatile lineman who can start day one at tackle or guard.

name: Jermod McCoy
risks: Missed entire 2025 season with ACL tear
summary: Was considered the top corner prospect before tearing his ACL in January 2025. High-ceiling talent with ideal size and athleticism if the knee checks out medically.
fitReason: Teams willing to bet on elite CB talent at a potential discount due to ACL recovery timeline.

name: Emmanuel McNeil-Warren
risks: Needs to prove against NFL-caliber athletes
summary: Small-school safety who has risen up draft boards with elite athleticism and instinctive play. A top-5 safety prospect in the class.
fitReason: Teams needing a safety who can range deep and make plays on the ball with versatile coverage skills.

name: Dillon Thieneman
risks: Can be challenged in run support
summary: Rangy safety with elite 4.35 speed who has risen to first-round territory after a dominant combine. Smart, instinctive player with ball-hawking ability.
fitReason: Teams needing a deep safety with range and playmaking ability to anchor the back end of the defense.

name: Omar Cooper Jr.
summary: Mendoza's top target who earned second-team All-American honors. Impressive combine (4.42 40, 37" vert) has solidified his first-round stock.
fitReason: Teams needing a speedy, productive WR who proved he can produce at a high level in a title-winning offense.

name: Blake Miller
strengths: Most pro-ready OT in the class
risks: Ceiling may be limited to solid starter
summary: The most pro-ready offensive tackle after Fano and Mauigoa. Consistent, technically sound pass protector who projects as a safe day-one starter.
fitReason: Teams that want a plug-and-play tackle who won't lose them games and provides a stable foundation.

name: Monroe Feeling
summary: Georgia-developed offensive tackle with good feet and balance. Benefits from the Bulldogs' elite offensive line tradition and coaching.
fitReason: Teams needing OL help in the late first round who value Georgia's proven OL development pipeline.

name: Jordyn Tyson
strengths: Essentially uncoverable in the red zone
summary: Dominant red-zone threat with the rare ability to win contested catches even when fully blanketed. Ideal size-speed combination for an X receiver.
fitReason: Teams needing a big-bodied WR who can win in contested situations and dominate the red zone.

name: Akheem Mesidor
strengths: Quickness and athleticism off the edge
summary: Quick and athletic pass rusher with 10.5 sacks and 4 forced fumbles in 2025. Uses hand technique and burst to consistently beat offensive tackles.
fitReason: Teams needing an EDGE rusher with proven college production and the quickness to generate pressure.

name: Kadyn Proctor
risks: Heavy and may struggle with speed rushers
summary: Massive left tackle prospect at 6'7" 352 who dominates in the run game. Alabama pedigree with starting experience against elite SEC competition.
fitReason: Teams needing a massive left tackle who can anchor the run game and has experience against top-tier competition.

name: Caleb Lomu
risks: May be limited to right tackle
summary: Another strong offensive tackle from Utah's elite OL pipeline. Reliable pass protector with a strong anchor who projects as a starting right tackle.
fitReason: Teams needing OL depth in the late first or early second round who value Utah's OL development track record.

name: Denzel Boston
strengths: Exceptional ball skills and contested catches
risks: May struggle to separate against press
summary: Big-bodied receiver with tremendous ball skills who hauled in 22 of 35 contested catch targets over two seasons. A physical pass catcher who wins at the catch point.
fitReason: Teams needing a physical, big-bodied WR who can win contested catches and provide a reliable target in the red zone.

name: CJ Allen
summary: Instinctive Georgia linebacker with sideline-to-sideline range and elite run defense. Benefits from the Bulldogs' proven LB development pipeline.
fitReason: Teams needing a three-down linebacker who can be the defensive signal-caller and stuff the run.

name: T.J. Parker
strengths: Ideal size and length for EDGE
risks: Production may not match physical tools yet
summary: Long, explosive edge rusher from Clemson with ideal physical tools for the position. Still developing his pass-rush repertoire but has top-10 upside.
fitReason: Teams needing EDGE help who are willing to develop a physically gifted prospect into a dominant pass rusher.

name: Cashius Howell
risks: Needs to improve against the run
summary: Explosive edge rusher from Texas A&M tested against elite SEC offensive lines. Ranked as a top-5 EDGE prospect in the class.
fitReason: Teams needing pass-rush help who want an SEC-tested edge player with explosive upside.

name: Colton Hood
risks: Speed concerns at the next level
summary: Physical, SEC-tested cornerback who combines solid coverage skills with willingness to tackle. 8 pass breakups and reliable run support.
fitReason: Teams needing a physical corner who can play press-man and contribute in run support on early downs.

name: Avieon Terrell
summary: Competitive, ball-disrupting corner with 9 pass breakups and 5 forced fumbles in 2025. All-American with the aggressiveness teams covet.
fitReason: Teams needing a competitive corner who forces turnovers and plays with aggression despite undersized frame.

name: KC Concepcion
summary: Smooth receiver from Texas A&M with good size and reliable hands. Projects as a solid WR2 at the next level.
fitReason: Teams looking for a second-round WR who can contribute immediately as a complementary piece.

name: Lee Hunter
strengths: Disruptive at the point of attack
summary: Massive interior defensive lineman who anchors against the run and collapses the pocket. One of the top DL prospects in the class.
fitReason: Teams needing an interior run-stuffer who can command double teams and free up linebackers.

name: Peter Woods
summary: ESPN's top-ranked defensive lineman in the class. Quick, penetrating interior defender who disrupts both run and pass plays from the inside.
fitReason: Teams that need an interior DL who can generate push and penetrate as a three-technique.

name: Caleb Banks
risks: May be a two-down player early
summary: Gigantic interior lineman with the frame to dominate at the point of attack. Still developing but the physical tools are rare.
fitReason: Teams needing a massive interior presence who can eat blocks and anchor the defensive front.

name: Anthony Hill Jr.
risks: May be limited to two-down role
summary: Physical, downhill linebacker from Texas who thrives in run defense and as a blitzer. SEC competition has prepared him for NFL physicality.
fitReason: Teams needing a physical linebacker who can stuff the run and rush the passer on designed blitzes.

name: Ty Simpson
strengths: Led SEC in passing yards and TDs
summary: Alabama QB who led the SEC in passing yards (3,500) and TDs (28). Fast processor with mobility, but a second-half fade in 2025 raises consistency concerns.
fitReason: QB-needy teams in the late first round who want a mobile, intelligent QB with SEC pedigree at a potential value.

name: Kayden McDonald
strengths: Big Ten Defensive Lineman of the Year
summary: Big Ten Defensive Lineman of the Year with a breakout 2025 season at Ohio State. Interior disruptor with pass-rush upside.
fitReason: Teams needing interior DL help who value a player with a breakout season and Ohio State pedigree.

name: Jadarian Price
summary: Nuanced running back with outstanding vision and natural instincts. Overshadowed by Jeremiyah Love at Notre Dame but has standalone RB1 potential.
fitReason: Teams needing an RB in round 2 who want a natural runner with vision and instincts at a value pick.

name: Zion Young
summary: Explosive Missouri edge rusher with bend and burst off the edge. SEC-tested with upside as a rotational pass rusher who can develop into a starter.
fitReason: Teams needing EDGE depth in the second round who want a pass-rush specialist with developmental upside.

name: Chris Johnson
summary: Small-school corner with good size and ball skills. Will need to prove he can adjust to NFL-level speed and competition.
fitReason: Teams looking for CB value in round 2 from a physical, ball-hawking corner with upside.

name: Malachi Lawrence
summary: Athletic UCF edge rusher with the versatility and quickness to contribute as a rotational pass rusher early in his career.
fitReason: Teams that need EDGE depth and are willing to develop a raw but athletic prospect.

name: Brandon Cisse
summary: Athletic SEC corner with a 41-inch vertical and good length. The physical tools are elite even if the production hasn't fully matched yet.
fitReason: Teams looking for an athletic CB in round 2 whose physical tools suggest higher upside than his college stats indicate.

name: Jacob Rodriguez
summary: Instinctive linebacker from Texas Tech who excels against the run and makes plays sideline to sideline. Solid second-round value.
fitReason: Teams needing LB depth who want a reliable run defender and tackler in the middle rounds.

name: R Mason Thomas
summary: Explosive Oklahoma edge rusher with length and bend. Projects as a pass-rush specialist with the tools to develop into an every-down player.
fitReason: Teams that need rotational EDGE help and want a high-upside developmental prospect.

name: Keionte Scott
risks: May be limited to nickel role
summary: Versatile nickel corner with rare blitzing ability — 5 sacks and 13 TFLs from the cornerback position. A unique defensive weapon.
fitReason: Teams needing a versatile nickel defender who can blitz, tackle, and cover in a multi-faceted defensive scheme.

name: Antonio Williams
summary: Reliable Clemson receiver with route precision and consistent hands. Projects as a solid WR2/WR3 at the next level.
fitReason: Teams looking for a reliable, ready-to-contribute WR in the second round.

name: Chase Bisontis
strengths: Looks and moves like a tackle at guard
risks: May not be powerful enough for gap schemes
summary: Athletic guard who moves like a tackle. Ideal fit in a zone-blocking scheme with the quickness and agility to reach defenders at the second level.
fitReason: Teams running zone-blocking schemes who need an athletic interior lineman who can get to the second level.

name: Germie Bernard
comparisonPlayer: Mecole Hardman with better route running
summary: Alabama receiver with good size and speed who projects as a vertical threat at the next level. Benefits from Bama's WR development pipeline.
fitReason: Teams needing a speedy vertical WR in the second round with Alabama pedigree and development ceiling.

name: Jalon Kilgore
summary: Versatile hybrid defender who can play safety and drop into the box. Projected as a Day 2 pick with scheme-dependent value.
fitReason: Teams that deploy hybrid safety/LB roles and need a versatile coverage weapon in the secondary.

name: Kyle Louis
summary: Instinctive Pittsburgh linebacker with coverage ability. Good value pick in the second round for teams needing a three-down LB.
fitReason: Teams needing a coverage-capable linebacker who can play all three downs.

name: Mike Washington Jr.
risks: Fumbling issues (8 in last two seasons)
May be a rotational back early
summary: Explosive, big-framed back who ran the fastest 40 among RBs at the combine (4.33). One-cut style with breakaway speed, though fumbling needs to be cleaned up.
fitReason: Teams needing a physical, fast RB who can complement an existing backfield or develop into a lead back.

name: Luke Hasz
strengths: Top-10 PFF receiving grade among all TEs
risks: Not the same athletic freak as Sadiq
summary: Reliable SEC tight end with an 82.3 PFF receiving grade — top 10 among all college TEs. Natural pass catcher who projects as a solid TE2 with starter upside.
fitReason: Teams needing a reliable receiving TE in the middle rounds with proven SEC production.
